concept: Sew / Stitch / Join with Needle and Thread
description: To join materials by passing thread through them with a needle—the fundamental act of creating garments, repairing tears, and connecting separate pieces into unified cloth. 'Pina' is precise, repetitive, meditative work that transforms fragments into whole.
sound_symbolism: The word is small and pointed like the needle itself. 'Pi' pierces with bilabial precision, the needle entering. The high 'i' is the thread's fine line. Then 'na' draws through with nasal continuity, stitch following stitch.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia wetha pina' (I sew cloth). Can name the garment made or mended: 'mia wethalu pina' (I sew a garment). Related to selomi (weave) but involves piercing and thread rather than interlacing.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Sewing repairs rather than discards, joins rather than separates. The stitcher practices patience and restoration.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sewing is intimate making—clothing the body, mending what is torn, connecting with tiny repeated acts of care.
